title: Reservoir Gauge Network: Maximize the Smallest Sensing Triangle in a Triangular Catchment
strategies: thin inscribed elliptical arc: drop every gauge on one flat ellipse (reproduces the checker baseline, ~0.1)
rounder inscribed ellipse: fatten the ring so every triple of gauges spans more area

fatter ellipse seed + seeded monotone hill-climb: jitter one gauge at a time inside the triangle, keep a move only if the minimum triangle area does not shrink, letting gauges drift into the corners a smooth ellipse wastes
Heilbronn-style dispersion / simulated-annealing placements that push gauges toward the three catchment corners
